title: Oral history: interview, narrator and interviewer
audience: Oral history projects, community archives, memory institutions
intro: An oral history interview has two people behind it — the narrator who tells the story and the interviewer who elicits it — plus a recording. This scenario branches on single-interview vs project, then attaches both people as Agents and the audio as an Instantiation.
question: Is an interview a Record? How do I record both the narrator and the interviewer, and the audio file?

steps.prompt: One interview, or a whole oral-history project?

choices.label: A single interview
choices.why: Good — one interview is one Record.

choices.label: An oral-history project
choices.why: Good — a project of many interviews is a Record Set, each interview a member Record.

choices.label: An Activity
choices.why: Close, but the interview's recorded content is a Record. The act of interviewing could be modelled as an Activity, but start with the record.

steps.prompt: Model the single interview (the recorded testimony).

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Fits. The testimony is a Record. The audio file is an Instantiation; narrator and interviewer are related Agents.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: That's the audio file, not the testimony's content. Keep the interview as a Record.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Too much for one interview — a Set is for the project.

capture.comment: Create the interview as a Record.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Fits. The project aggregates many interviews — a Record Set — each a member Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Undersells it — a project is many interviews with different narrators. Use a Record Set.

choices.label: Activity
choices.why: The project of recording could be framed as an Activity, but the aggregation of interviews is a Record Set.

capture.comment: Create the project as a Record Set.

steps.prompt: Attach the narrator — the person whose testimony this is.

choices.label: Agent (Person) + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. The narrator is a Person (an Agent) and is a creator of the testimony's content. Relate the interview to them with has_creator; reuse the Agent for any other interviews they gave.

choices.label: Put the name in the title
choices.why: Doesn't fit. Narrators should be reconcilable Agents so their testimony can be found and (where consent allows) linked.

capture.comment: Create the narrator as an Agent.
Link the interview to the narrator with has_creator.

steps.prompt: Now the interviewer — does an interview have one creator, or two?

choices.label: Also an Agent + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. RiC lets a record have several creators. The interviewer shaped the testimony too, so relate them to the interview with has_creator as well — both narrator and interviewer become reusable Agents.

choices.label: Only the narrator counts
choices.why: Misses something. The interviewer's questions shape the record; capturing them as a creator makes the interview discoverable from the interviewer's side too.

capture.comment: Create the interviewer as an Agent.
Link the interview to the interviewer with has_creator.

choices.label: Instantiation
choices.why: Fits. The WAV/MP3 is an Instantiation of the interview Record, linked with has_instantiation. A preservation master and an access copy are simply two Instantiations of the same interview.

choices.label: A separate Record
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The file isn't a different interview — it's a carrier of this one. Model it as an Instantiation.

choices.label: An attribute on the interview
choices.why: Too flat — files have format, duration and fixity, and there are usually several. Use Instantiations.

capture.comment: Create the audio file as an Instantiation.
Link the interview to its audio with has_instantiation.

outcome.verdict: Testimony, both voices, and the recording — linked.
outcome.summary: The interview is a Record (or the project a Record Set); narrator and interviewer are both reusable Agents related by has_creator; and the audio is an Instantiation. You can now find every interview a narrator gave, everything an interviewer recorded, and every carrier of a given testimony.

links.label: See real RiC data in the graph viewer
